issues.title: Daily Log page missing legally defensible form structure with weather auto-population, crew tracking, and submit workflow
issues.description: The Daily Log page lacks the P0-4 speed-optimized form: no weather auto-populate from API, no 'Same as yesterday' crew clone, no section toggles for incidents/visitors, no voice-to-text, no draft/submitted/approved status workflow. Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 7, a superintendent must submit a complete log in under 90 seconds. Currently the page shows mock data cards with no real form.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, completely rebuild the DailyLog component to implement the P0-4 Daily Log Speed Optimization. BEFORE: The page renders static mock data cards and a basic table with no real form entry, no weather auto-population, no crew cloning, no submission workflow. AFTER: Implement a production daily log form with these sections: (1) Header with date picker (default today) and project name; (2) Weather section that auto-populates temperature, conditions, wind from the weather_cache table via useWeather hook (show 'Auto' badge on auto-filled fields, allow override); (3) 'Same as yesterday' button that clones crew entries, equipment, and visitors from the previous day's log; (4) Manpower table (trade/company/headcount/hours with running totals); (5) Work Performed rich text area with link to schedule phases; (6) 'No incidents today' toggle (default ON, expands incident form only when toggled OFF); (7) 'No visitors today' toggle (default ON, expands visitor list only when toggled OFF); (8) Equipment section pre-filled from yesterday; (9) Photo batch upload section with thumbnail grid; (10) Sticky 'Submit Log' button at bottom. Use status workflow: draft -> submitted -> approved. Query daily_logs table from Supabase for existing entries. Show calendar view with green/yellow/orange/red dots for approved/submitted/draft/missing days. Empty state: 'No daily logs yet. The daily log is your project official record.' with 'Start Today Log' CTA. Add persistent banner when today has no log: 'Today daily log has not been started' with Start Log button. Use theme tokens from src/styles/theme.ts for all styling. All metric cards at top: Total Logs, This Week, Avg Crew Size, Days Without Incident. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Safety page missing OSHA-compliant incident tracking, TRIR calculation, inspection templates, and certification expiry tracking
issues.description: Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 11, the Safety page must show Days Since Last Incident counter, TRIR calculation, open corrective actions, inspection schedule, certification expiry alerts, toolbox talk attendance tracking, and OSHA 300/300A form generation. The current page has placeholder content with no real safety workflow.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, rebuild the Safety component to implement OSHA-compliant safety management. BEFORE: The page shows basic placeholder content with mock safety data and no real incident tracking, no TRIR calculation, no inspection workflow. AFTER: Implement a production safety dashboard with: (1) Metric cards row: Days Since Last Incident (prominent counter, resets on medical_treatment+ severity), TRIR calculated as (recordable_incidents * 200000 / total_hours_worked) with industry benchmark comparison, Open Corrective Actions count with overdue badge, Inspections This Week, Certifications Expiring Soon (within 30 days); (2) Incident Log tab with table columns: Date, Type (injury/near_miss/property_damage/environmental), Severity with color coding (first_aid=yellow, medical_treatment=orange, lost_time=red, fatality=black per OSHA standards), Location, Involved Parties, Status (open/investigating/closed), Corrective Actions count; (3) Incident creation modal requiring: date, location, description, severity, involved persons, root cause, corrective actions with assignee and due date; (4) Toolbox Talks tab: list of talks with topic, date, presenter, attendee count, digital sign-in capability; (5) Certifications tab: personnel certifications with expiration tracking, alerts at 90/60/30 days before expiry, expired shown in red with EXPIRED badge; (6) Inspections tab with configurable checklist templates (daily safety walk, weekly site audit, monthly equipment inspection), each item pass/fail/NA with photo and note fields. Query incidents, safety_inspections, safety_certifications, toolbox_talks, corrective_actions tables from Supabase. Empty state: 'Safety tracking not yet set up. Configure your safety program to track incidents, inspections, and certifications.' with 'Set Up Safety Program' CTA. Use status dots: green=resolved, amber=investigating, red=open. All styling via theme tokens. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Field Capture page missing camera-first workflow, GPS auto-tagging, offline queue, and AI photo analysis
issues.description: Per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 14, Field Capture must be camera-first: one tap opens camera, auto-tags with GPS and timestamp, post-capture form is optional, works offline with sync queue. Currently the page has no camera integration, no GPS tagging, no offline support, and no AI photo suggestions.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, rebuild the FieldCapture component as a camera-first mobile-optimized capture tool. BEFORE: The page shows a basic grid of placeholder photos with no camera integration, no GPS tagging, no offline queue, no linking to drawings/tasks/punch items. AFTER: Implement a production field capture interface with: (1) Prominent 'Capture' button (64px tall, full width on mobile, orange background) that opens native camera via file input with accept='image/*' and capture='environment'; (2) Auto-metadata on every capture: GPS coordinates via navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition, timestamp from Date.now(), device info from navigator.userAgent; (3) Post-capture overlay form (appears after photo taken): Title (optional), Notes (optional with microphone icon for voice-to-text placeholder), Tags multi-select (progress/safety/quality/defect/delivery), Link To searchable dropdown (drawing/task/punch_item/rfi/daily_log) all fields optional so user can 'Save' with just the photo; (4) Photo feed view: chronological grid of captured photos with metadata overlays showing date, time, location name, tags as colored pills; (5) Map view toggle: photos plotted on project site by GPS coordinates using a simple list-based location view; (6) Offline banner: 'You are offline, changes will sync when you reconnect' with queue count 'X photos pending upload'; (7) Bulk operations: select multiple photos to add tags, link to item, export, or delete; (8) Metric cards at top: Total Captures, This Week, Untagged, Pending Sync. Query field_captures or files table from Supabase filtered by category='photo'. Store captures in IndexedDB when offline. Empty state: 'No field captures yet. Start documenting site conditions with photos.' with 'Open Camera' CTA. Large touch targets (44px minimum). Use theme tokens for all styling. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: All field operations pages use hardcoded mock data instead of Supabase queries with real-time subscriptions
issues.description: Per FOUNDER PRIORITIES P0, all pages must query Supabase with real-time subscriptions. The daily log, safety, and field capture pages all render hardcoded arrays. No useSupabase hooks exist for daily_logs, incidents, safety_inspections, toolbox_talks, corrective_actions, or field captures. Empty database shows no helpful empty states.
issues.prompt: Create the file src/hooks/useFieldOperations.ts with production Supabase hooks for all field operations entities. BEFORE: This file does not exist. Daily log, safety, and field capture pages use inline mock data arrays. AFTER: Create a new file exporting these hooks following the exact pattern from CODEBASE_PATTERNS.md (useCallback for fetch, useState for state, useEffect for subscription): (1) useDailyLogs(projectId: string) querying daily_logs table with fields: id, project_id, log_date, weather_description, temperature_high, temperature_low, weather_notes, site_visit_notes, safety_incidents, safety_notes, visitor_count, created_by, created_at, updated_at. Include real-time subscription on postgres_changes for table daily_logs filtered by project_id. Return { logs, isLoading, error, refetch, createLog, updateLog }. (2) useIncidents(projectId: string) querying incidents table. Return { incidents, isLoading, error, refetch, createIncident, updateIncident }. (3) useSafetyInspections(projectId: string) querying safety_inspections table. Return { inspections, isLoading, error, refetch }. (4) useToolboxTalks(projectId: string) querying toolbox_talks table. Return { talks, isLoading, error, refetch, createTalk }. (5) useCorrectiveActions(projectId: string) querying corrective_actions table. Return { actions, isLoading, error, refetch, createAction, updateAction }. (6) useWeatherCache(projectId: string) querying weather_records table for latest record. Return { weather, isLoading, error }. (7) useSafetyCertifications(projectId: string) querying safety_certifications table. Return { certifications, isLoading, error, refetch }. Import supabase from '../lib/supabase'. Use Database types from '../types/database' where available, otherwise use inline interfaces. Each hook must handle loading state, error state, and empty state. All mutations should use optimistic patterns: update local state immediately, revert on error with console.error. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: ActivityCard and MentionInput components missing ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and focus management
issues.description: The ActivityCard component renders interactive elements (entity links, comment buttons) without proper ARIA roles, keyboard event handlers, or focus indicators. The MentionInput dropdown has no aria-listbox role, no aria-activedescendant for keyboard selection, and no screen reader announcements for mention suggestions. Per Global Acceptance Standards, all pages must pass axe-core with zero critical/serious violations.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/activity/ActivityCard.tsx, add comprehensive accessibility support to the ActivityCard component and its sub-components. BEFORE: The EntityLabel button has an aria-label but the parent card div has no role or keyboard handler. The card uses onClick but is a div, not a button or link, making it inaccessible to keyboard users. Comment and bookmark buttons lack aria-labels. No focus ring visible on interactive elements. AFTER: (1) Add role='article' and aria-label to the main card container describing the activity (e.g., 'Activity: {user} {action} {target}'); (2) Make the card focusable with tabIndex={0} and add onKeyDown handler that triggers onClick on Enter/Space; (3) Add visible focus ring style: outline: '2px solid ' + colors.primaryOrange with outlineOffset: '2px' on :focus-visible (use onFocus/onBlur state to toggle); (4) Add aria-label to the bookmark button: 'Bookmark this activity'; (5) Add aria-label to the comment button: 'Add comment, {commentCount} existing comments'; (6) Add aria-label to the like/thumbs-up button: 'Like this activity'; (7) Ensure all icon-only buttons have minimum 44x44px touch target by adding minWidth: 44, minHeight: 44 to their styles; (8) Add aria-live='polite' to the time display so screen readers announce time updates; (9) Add role='img' and aria-label to the type icon badge describing the activity type. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features.title: AI Safety Photo Scanner
invented_features.description: When field photos are captured, an edge function analyzes them via Claude vision API to detect PPE violations (missing hard hats, safety vests, eye protection), unsafe scaffolding, fall hazards, and housekeeping issues. Results appear as suggestions on the photo with confidence scores. Superintendent can confirm or dismiss each finding. Confirmed violations auto-create corrective actions with assignee and due date. This replaces manual safety observation walks for routine PPE compliance.

invented_features.title: Voice-to-Daily-Log
invented_features.description: Superintendent taps a microphone button on the daily log page and speaks naturally: 'Concrete crew poured 200 yards on grid lines A through F, had 12 guys on site, no incidents, weather was clear and 72 degrees.' An edge function transcribes via Whisper API, then Claude parses the transcript into structured daily log fields (crew count, work performed, weather, incidents). The form auto-fills and superintendent reviews before submitting. Target: complete daily log in under 30 seconds.

competitive_intel: Procore Quick Capture allows video-to-punch-item with automatic transcription and one-tap photo capture from the field. Fieldwire's mobile-first approach opens directly to the project page with swipe gestures and Apple Pencil markup, plus full offline functionality with auto-sync. OpenSpace uses helmet cameras plus computer vision for automated progress tracking, claiming 50% delay reduction. SiteSync field operations currently has no camera integration, no offline support, no GPS tagging, and no voice input, putting it behind all three competitors in the core field workflow. The daily log lacks the speed optimizations (same-as-yesterday, section toggles, auto-weather) that would make it faster than paper. Safety module has no OSHA form generation, no TRIR calculation, and no certification tracking, which are table stakes for any GC with an EMR above 0.8.
summary: Field Operations is the weakest module in SiteSync at 16/100, critically lacking the three core field workflows that superintendents use daily: a fast daily log form (target: 90 seconds), a camera-first field capture tool with GPS tagging and offline support, and OSHA-compliant safety tracking with incident management and TRIR calculations. All three pages render hardcoded mock data with no Supabase integration, no real-time subscriptions, and no meaningful empty states. The five issues target: (1) rebuilding the Daily Log with weather auto-population, crew cloning, section toggles, and draft/submitted/approved workflow per P0-4 spec; (2) implementing the Safety page with OSHA-compliant incident tracking, TRIR calculation, inspection templates, toolbox talks, and certification expiry tracking; (3) building a camera-first Field Capture page with GPS auto-tagging, offline queue, and entity linking; (4) creating production Supabase hooks for all field operations entities with real-time subscriptions; (5) adding comprehensive accessibility to the shared ActivityCard and MentionInput components. These changes directly address the three lowest-scoring dimensions: data richness (14), AI integration (8), and mobile-first (12), while building the P0-4 Daily Log Speed Optimization from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP.md.
